ground_truth.title: 10 Best Moving Companies in New York (2025 Reviews)
ground_truth.author: Ryan Carrigan
ground_truth.main_content:
Hi, I'm Ryan. Let's get moving.

I've helped thousands of people move over the last decade, and I can tell you that no two moves are exactly alike. Let me help you find the mover that's right for you.

Best local NY mover: Zip to Zip Moving Inc
Best interstate mover: Safeway Moving
Best moving container: PODS
Best labor-only mover: Zip to Zip Moving Inc
Best rental truck: Budget Truck Rental

Our track record

Each year, 400,000+ people trust our moving recommendations. Here are a few reasons why:

- Founded in 2015
- 3,500+ moving companies analyzed
- $50,000 in moving grants delivered
- Up-to-date pricing info & industry data
- Fact-checked for accuracy

Best movers for those moving locally in New York

Whether you're moving across town in New York City, Brooklyn, or elsewhere in New York — or within 50 miles down the road — hiring a local NY mover is likely your best bet.

We've evaluated 133 local New York movers and recommend Zip to Zip Moving Inc thanks to their professionalism and clear rates.

1. Zip to Zip Moving Inc: 4.58 out of 5 stars
2. Lifestyle Moving & Storage: 4.12 out of 5 stars
3. Men On The Move: 4.76 out of 5 stars
4. Seka Moving: 4.7 out of 5 stars
5. Piece of Cake Moving & Storage: 4.66 out of 5 stars

Zip to Zip Moving Inc

Address: 218 Withers St Apt. 1A, Brooklyn, NY 11211

Rating: 4.58/5

98% of all reviews were positive

1% of reviews were negative

Review Analysis

Fast moves, friendly faces—Zip to Zip Moving Inc receives high marks from 722 customer reviews, with over 98% positive input. Most feedback highlights efficient service, polite and professional staff, careful handling of property, a quick moving process, and helpful movers. Many users describe the staff as respectful and attentive, while pointing to smooth, fast, and damage-free moves as standout qualities. A handful of reviews mention issues such as significantly higher-than-expected final costs, property damage, and slow or insufficient responses to complaints. Compared to other moving services, Zip to Zip stands out for speed and courtesy, but potential clients should discuss all pricing details and coverage for damages upfront. For most, the experience is seamless and stress-free; only a few customers report major concerns.

Lifestyle Moving & Storage

Address: 99 Hudson St, 5th Floor #7148, New York, NY 10013

Rating: 4.12/5

97% of all reviews were positive

2% of reviews were negative

Review Analysis

This moving company stands out with consistently high marks: 97% of the 376 feedback entries are positive. Customers frequently mention that the team operates with impressive efficiency, professionalism, and politeness. Movers handle items carefully, complete moves quickly, and keep clients informed at each step. Communication, from booking through the final check-in, is often cited as one of the best customers have experienced. By contrast, a small percentage of input highlights damage to property or belongings, occasional rushed jobs, and rare lapses in communication. Some reviewers mention unresolved claims or dissatisfied complaint handling, particularly for moves involving damages. Compared to many moving companies in the region, Lifestyle Moving & Storage draws praise for reliability, cost-effectiveness, and standout customer service. Most customer input agrees: their streamlined, attentive approach makes moving less stressful than competitors.

Men On The Move

Address: 150 Crossways Park Drive West, Woodbury, NY 11797

Rating: 4.76/5

85% of all reviews were positive

12% of reviews were negative

Review Analysis

Customers rave about Men On The Move for being efficient, professional, and polite. Most feedback highlights their careful handling of belongings, quick process, and competitive rates. Many describe a stress-free experience with friendly, respectful staff and transparent communication. Issues that do arise are usually resolved quickly, setting them apart from many moving companies. Some customers report problems with damaged items, unexpected or higher-than-estimated charges, and a few mention rude staff interactions. These complaints, though less common, show that while Men On The Move consistently delivers top service for most, a small number have faced notable setbacks. With 682 reviews analyzed—85% positive—Men On The Move stands out with more reliable service, better staff, and smoother moves compared to other movers in the area.

Men On The Move, serving Woodbury, NY since 1988, specializes in local residential moving services and has cultivated a reputation for reliability and professionalism. They also offer packing, unpacking, and crating services, ensuring a comprehensive moving experience. Highly regarded by customers, Men On The Move boasts an impressive 4.0 average rating from 228 Yelp reviews and a near-perfect 4.9 rating from 68 Google reviews, complemented by an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau. Unlike many nearby competitors, they also provide full valuation insurance coverage, offering additional peace of mind to those entrusting them with valuable items like pianos, art, and antiques.

Seka Moving

Address: 48 Wall St #1100, New York, NY 10005

Rating: 4.7/5

85% of all reviews were positive

13% of reviews were negative

Review Analysis

Seka Moving earns top marks from customers who praise efficient service, careful handling of belongings, professional and polite staff, speed, and clear communication. In 495 reviews, 85% are positive, suggesting that most find the company reliable and courteous. Feedback consistently highlights movers' attention to protecting property and working swiftly, even with challenging jobs or tight schedules. Customers appreciate that estimates and pricing tend to be accurate. Negative input accounts for 13%, where the main complaints center on damaged items, delayed arrival or delivery, and unexpected charges after the move. These issues can be especially frustrating during long-distance relocations, where delivery windows or extra fees sometimes catch people off guard. Neutral reviews typically note reasonable moves but cite problems like slow packing or unclear fee explanations. Compared to many movers, Seka stands out for its organization, efficiency, and respectful treatment of customers' property, but like others in this industry, a few operational or scheduling missteps sometimes occur. The company's strength lies in its fast, attentive, and careful crews, making it a leading choice among moving services, especially for local moves.

Seka Moving has been providing local residential moving services in and around New York, NY since 2019 and has quickly built a reputable name in the industry. With impressive consumer reviews—an average rating of 4.6 on Yelp and 4.9 on Google My Business, coupled with an A+ Better Business Bureau rating—Seka Moving stands out among nearby competitors. Their comprehensive offerings include packing and unpacking services, labor-only services, and specialized moving for items such as pianos, hot tubs, and art. Though a deposit is required, they accept multiple payment forms and provide full valuation insurance coverage, making them a trustworthy choice for local moves.

Piece of Cake Moving & Storage

Address: 287 Park Avenue South, New York, NY 10010 US

Rating: 4.66/5

95% of all reviews were positive

5% of reviews were negative

Review Analysis

Piece of Cake Moving & Storage makes relocating feel like a breeze for most customers, with 94.5% of nearly 9,000 reviews giving positive feedback. Their crews often win praise for being efficient, friendly, and showing care with belongings, making moves run quickly and protecting furniture well. Many also mention good communication and stress-free experiences, even for moves under tough conditions. Some reviewers highlight organized packing and transparent pricing as standout qualities that set Piece of Cake above their competition in major cities. Still, a small but notable group (5%) describe frustrating experiences such as item damage, surprise charges not made clear upfront, or missing boxes—and a handful share stories of items lost or mishandled during long-distance moves. A few neutral reviews point out that while customer support is responsive, the packing service can fall short of expectations. Compared to similar companies, Piece of Cake is recognized for consistent service, but it's important for customers to ensure item lists are accurate and to monitor the moving process. For those seeking a smooth, hands-off move with careful, reliable movers, Piece of Cake comes highly recommended, but vigilance is wise to prevent issues common in the industry.

Piece of Cake Moving & Storage offers top-tier local residential moving services in and around New York, NY, setting itself apart from competitors with its reliability and efficiency. Since 2019, this company has garnered a stellar reputation, boasting an impressive 4.7 average rating on Yelp from 1,580 reviews and a 4.8 average rating on Google from 3,214 reviews. They also hold a BBB rating of A-, underscoring their commitment to customer satisfaction. In addition to local moves, their comprehensive services include packing, unpacking, and specialty moves for pianos, art, and antiques. Customers appreciate that they accept credit cards, although a deposit is required.

Best moving containers in New York

Need a budget-friendly move, extra time, or storage along the way? Moving containers are a flexible DIY option that can also save you lots of money. You can pack at your own pace over several days, and once you're set, the company will handle the transport for you.

Here's a look at our top moving container choices in New York:

Best rental trucks for your New York move

Opting for a rental truck is one of the most economical moving methods — but it requires the most hands-on work. You'll need to manage everything from start to finish, including driving. If you're willing to put in the labor, it can result in major savings, particularly for local moves.

Rental trucks can also be used for cross-country moves, but don't overlook hidden costs like fuel and per-mile fees. These extras can really add up, so take them into account when budgeting.

Check out our picks for best rental moving trucks in New York:

Labor-only hourly movers in New York

If you're cool with driving but need help lifting and loading, labor-only movers are a great option. They'll tackle the packing and heavy lifting while you stay in control of the rental and the route.

Take a look at these highly recommended labor-only movers for your New York move:

How we chose the best moving companies in New York

moveBuddha's expert team analyzed 133 New York movers and listed the top movers based on customer satisfaction, reviews, and cost.

Customer satisfaction (40%): The company consistently delivers services in a timely matter with good communication and within the estimated cost.

Dispute resolution (20%): Mistakes cannot be avoided in the moving industry. We look for companies that own their mistakes and make genuine efforts to fix any issues that arise during a move.

Industry reputation (15%): The company is well-regarded within the moving industry and participates in industry conferences and organizations.

Online reputation (15%): The company maintains a professional website with accurate information. The company promptly and professionally responds to online customer inquiries.

Service options (10%): We look for companies with a deep breadth of services including full packing, storage, specialty item handling, and expedited delivery.

Average cost of moving companies in New York

The table below includes estimated moving costs for local relocations within New York. For a deeper dive into pricing, here's our guide on the cost to move in New York.

Remember, the figures shown are averages. Your actual moving costs may vary significantly based on the details of your move and whether the movers use flat-rate pricing.

You can also use our free moving cost calculator to get a more personalized estimate.

| Home size | Avg. total cost | Cost/hour | # of movers | # hours |
| Studio | $465 | $183 | 2 movers | 3 hours |
| 1 bedroom | $648 | $183 | 2 movers | 4 hours |
| 2 bedroom | $1,052 | $257 | 3 movers | 5 hours |
| 3 bedroom | $2,438 | $359 | 4 movers | 8 hours |
| 4 bedroom | $2,797 | $359 | 4 movers | 9 hours |
| 5+ bedroom | $4,304 | $503 | 5 movers | 10 hours |

- On average, moving a studio within New York costs about $465.
- Moving a one-bedroom apartment locally in New York will cost around $648.
- Two-bedroom moves in New York come in at approximately $1,052.
- The typical cost of moving a three-bedroom home in New York is $2,438.
- A four-bedroom move in New York generally runs about $2,797.
- Need to move a five-bedroom or larger house in New York? Expect to spend about $4,304.

Factors that affect moving costs in New York

An online moving quote doesn't always tell the full story, so keep these factors in mind when planning your New York move.

- Distance: Fuel and time can impact local moves, and out-of-state relocations may bring added expenses.
- Size: The more items and space you have, the more you'll pay in labor and truck size.
- Time of year: In New York, the busy moving seasons like summer and holidays often come with premium rates.
- Additional services: Tasks like packing or furniture setup take time and add to your moving costs.

Things to keep in mind when moving

No matter if you're heading to a nearby city in New York or embarking on a long-distance cross-country journey, here are a few key things to consider:

- Weather: The climate of New York state is generally has warm to hot summers and cold winters. In the farther southeastern portion of the state (New York City and Long Island area) hot and humid summers and cool to mild winters. Winter temperatures average below freezing during January and February in much of New York state, but several degrees above freezing along the Atlantic coastline, including New York City.
- HOA guidelines: New York neighborhoods with HOAs often have specific moving rules regarding times, truck types, and parking. Reading the handbook ahead of time can help you avoid fees and friction with your new neighbors.
- Elevator logistics: In buildings with elevators, management may require you to reserve one in advance — usually a designated service elevator. They might also require you to adhere to specific move-in hours.
- Permits for parking: Though New York doesn't usually require special permits for moving vehicles, other cities or states might. It's best to check with your new municipality for parking details.
- Check mover credentials: The movers we suggest are licensed and insured, but you can also research them through the FMCSA database to view their safety record and compliance.
- USPS notification: To avoid mail interruptions, complete your change of address form with USPS at least one week before your move date.
- Moving coverage: Movers provide free basic coverage by law, but it's minimal. For expensive items, additional insurance may be a smart investment to protect against loss or damage.

FAQs

What should I consider when hiring a moving company in New York?

When selecting a moving company in New York, look for a strong track record, honest and upfront pricing, a wide range of services, and several insurance coverage choices. These elements — among others — are key to our reviews and rankings, helping you easily identify the best movers in New York.

Be sure to request quotes from at least three movers, including one of the best flat-rate companies in New York, to compare value and services.

Can New York movers handle relocations across state lines?

Yes. A strong share of movers in New York are qualified for interstate work. Of the 148 total movers in NY, 130 carry authorization to manage interstate relocations. This ensures plenty of choice if you need a mover with a USDOT number regulated by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration.

To find the most reliable fit, take a look at our picks for the best long-distance movers in New York.

How can I see all moving companies in New York?

You can see all moving companies in New York in our comprehensive list of New York movers.
